hooks.name: Start Dev Server on Task Complete
hooks.description: Automatically starts the dev server for the active Dev Mode workspace after a task completes successfully, and shares the localhost URL.

action.prompt:
When the task has completed successfully (you displayed the 🟩 DONE banner), start the dev server for the current Dev Mode workspace and share the localhost URL with the user.

Workspace paths:
- Dev Mode swf → cd /Users/rajeevnaik/Code/xConvert.com/scrabblewordsfinder && npm run dev (localhost:4321)
- Dev Mode xconvert → cd /Users/rajeevnaik/Code/xConvert.com && npm run dev (localhost:4321)
- Dev Mode coins → cd /Users/rajeevnaik/Code/xConvert.com/coins && npm run dev (localhost:4321)
- Dev Mode playground → cd /Users/rajeevnaik/Code/xConvert.com/playground && npm run dev (localhost:4321)

Steps:
1. Determine which workspace is currently in Dev Mode from the conversation context.
2. Start the dev server as a background process using the control_bash_process tool (action: start).
3. Wait a few seconds, then read the process output to confirm it started and get the URL.
4. Share the URL with the user: "Dev server running at http://localhost:4321"

If a dev server is already running (process reused), just share the URL without restarting.
If no Dev Mode is active, skip this step silently.
